title: Untrusted Markup, Formula Injection, Paths, Logs, and Plugin Boundaries
description: Extracted text becomes HTML, CSV formulas execute, URLs create paths, terminal controls poison logs, and third-party plugins receive raw secrets and network authority. Learners cumulatively produce safe untrusted-content processing boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Render findings through text-safe sinks or context-specific encoding and narrowly reviewed sanitization.
Preserve data while neutralizing spreadsheet formula triggers under an explicit export and consumer contract.
Generate owned filenames, confine paths, reject traversal and unsafe archive entries, and handle collisions and Unicode deliberately.
